steps.title: Coalesce and launch the Experiential Learning Community
steps.summary: Build on work already under way through the July-August and September-October planning windows to bring together the founding experiential learning community, clarify its initial priorities and deliver the confirmed September 2026 launch under interim institutional leadership.

outputs.title: Experiential Learning Community launch
outputs.summary: A September launch with a founding membership, initial priorities and a clear rhythm for continued participation.

steps.title: Develop and establish the governance, resourcing and faculty support model
steps.summary: Develop and establish the operating model from July through December 2026, including interim and funded convenorship, community support, funded projects, accountability and the indicative resource envelope. Work with faculties to agree the principles through which future community convenors can receive release, workload allocation or backfill support, including central and faculty contributions, eligibility, duration and review. Establish the Innovation Committee by December 2026 with an agreed remit, membership and authority to govern the communities, convenorship and funded activity.

outputs.title: Operational Innovation Committee
outputs.summary: A governing committee with an agreed remit, membership and authority to steward the communities, convenorship and funded activity.

outputs.title: Governance and resourcing model
outputs.summary: An agreed model covering convenorship, community support, funded projects, accountability and the indicative resource envelope.

outputs.title: Agreed release and backfill principles
outputs.summary: A sufficiently clear and equitable institutional offer to support the funded convenor call from January 2027.

steps.title: Confirm interim convenors and integrate the remaining communities
steps.summary: In the November-December planning window, identify existing institutional leaders to act as interim, unremitted convenors and use their leadership to bring the AI in Education and Inclusive Education communities into the shared institutional frame by December 2026.

outputs.title: Three communities operating in a common frame
outputs.summary: Experiential Learning, AI in Education and Inclusive Education each have interim leadership, a founding constituency and an initial programme of activity.

steps.title: Run funded convenor and community project calls
steps.summary: Launch coordinated calls for funded Community Convenors and community-aligned projects in January-February 2027, then complete selection, confirm faculty release and backfill arrangements, appoint convenors and agree the first funded project portfolio in March-April.

outputs.title: Open institutional calls
outputs.summary: Published calls with clear eligibility, support, selection criteria, expectations and decision routes.

outputs.title: Funded leadership and project portfolio
outputs.summary: Appointed convenors and an approved first set of projects that strengthen the communities and their shared work.

steps.title: Build the first three communities and establish their operating conventions
steps.summary: From January to August 2027, develop the Experiential Learning, AI in Education and Inclusive Education communities into active and recognisable communities of practice. Through their early work, establish and test the shared conventions for convenorship, participation, communication, collaborative inquiry, student and partner involvement, funded activity, decision-making and routes into institutional influence. This will create three functioning communities and a clear basis for how future strategic communities of practice can be established and supported at King's.

outputs.title: Three established communities with shared operating conventions
outputs.summary: Three communities with supported leadership, sustained participation and credible programmes of activity, alongside an agreed and tested set of conventions to guide future communities of practice.

steps.title: Operate the first three communities
steps.summary: Operate the Experiential Learning, AI in Education and Inclusive Education communities through the September 2027-June 2028 academic-year cycle. Support their programmes, funded projects, participation and routes into institutional influence while gathering consistent evidence of activity, outcomes and resource requirements.

outputs.title: First full operating cycle
outputs.summary: Three communities completing a coherent September-June cycle with supported leadership, visible activity and evidence of their contribution.

steps.title: Review the first three communities and agree the expansion portfolio
steps.summary: From September to December 2027, review the first communities alongside the opening months of their operating cycle. Assess participation, activity, governance, convenorship, faculty release and backfill, funding, project mechanisms, shared operating conventions and emerging institutional influence, then approve three additional strategic communities for 2028-29.

outputs.title: First impact and operating-model review
outputs.summary: An evidence-led assessment of the first three communities, with agreed refinements to governance, resourcing and operating arrangements.

outputs.title: Approved six-community portfolio
outputs.summary: Three additional strategic communities agreed for mobilisation and operation in the 2028-29 academic year.

steps.title: Confirm convenors, resources and launch the 2028-29 project call
steps.summary: In January-February 2028, confirm or recruit convenors for all six communities and secure the release, workload allocation, backfill and operational support required for the following academic year. Agree the community and project funding envelope and launch a coordinated institutional call for projects aligned to the approved portfolio.

outputs.title: Confirmed convenors and resource arrangements
outputs.summary: Leadership, faculty release or backfill and operational support agreed in time for the 2028-29 workload cycle.

outputs.title: Open 2028-29 project call
outputs.summary: An institutional call for projects aligned to the approved six-community portfolio, supported by an agreed funding envelope and selection process.

steps.title: Select projects and mobilise the expanded portfolio
steps.summary: From March to August 2028, assess and select community-aligned projects, confirm project leads, funding and delivery arrangements, and establish the three additional communities. Prepare all six communities, convenors and funded projects to begin the next operating cycle together in September 2028.

outputs.title: Approved 2028-29 funded project portfolio
outputs.summary: Selected projects with confirmed leads, funding, expectations and routes into the work of the six communities.

outputs.title: Expanded portfolio ready to operate
outputs.summary: Three additional communities established and all six communities, convenors and projects mobilised for September 2028.

steps.title: Operate the six-community portfolio
steps.summary: Operate all six strategic communities through the September 2028-June 2029 academic-year cycle, supporting their programmes, funded projects, institutional influence and cross-community learning while gathering consistent evidence of participation, outcomes and resource requirements.

outputs.title: Full six-community operating cycle
outputs.summary: Six communities completing a coherent September-June cycle, including the contribution of their funded projects.

steps.title: Review the six-community portfolio and agree the following year
steps.summary: From September to December 2028, review the six-community portfolio as the new operating cycle begins. Assess impact, effectiveness and sustainability, determine which communities should continue or evolve, and agree recommendations for recurrent resources, permanent governance and ownership, and the next annual commissioning cycle.

outputs.title: Second impact and operating-model review
outputs.summary: An assessment of the impact, effectiveness and sustainability of the expanded portfolio, with an agreed direction for the following academic year.

outputs.title: Recommended permanent institutional model
outputs.summary: Recommendations for the continuing portfolio, recurrent resources, governance, ownership and annual commissioning rhythm.

steps.title: Confirm BAU resourcing and the 2029-30 portfolio
steps.summary: In January-February 2029, approve the continuing portfolio and confirm convenors, release, backfill, central support and project funding for 2029-30. Agree the permanent institutional owner, governance route, budget holder, recurrent resource settlement and annual planning cycle in time for the following academic year's workload and budget decisions.

outputs.title: Approved BAU model and resource settlement
outputs.summary: A permanently owned, governed and recurrently resourced model, with the 2029-30 portfolio and leadership arrangements confirmed.

steps.title: Complete final mobilisation and transition preparations
steps.summary: From March to August 2029, complete any final commissioning and mobilisation required for the 2029-30 portfolio and prepare the transfer of governance, budgets, reporting, operational support and accountability to the permanent institutional owner.

outputs.title: Portfolio and handover ready for September
outputs.summary: The next annual portfolio is mobilised and the operational, governance and resource arrangements are ready to transfer into business as usual.

steps.title: Transition to business as usual
steps.summary: In September 2029, complete the transfer to the permanent institutional owner so that Strategic Communities of Practice continue through the established annual rhythm of September-June operation, autumn review, January-February resource confirmation and March-August mobilisation.

outputs.title: Business-as-usual transition complete
outputs.summary: Strategic Communities of Practice operating through permanent ownership, governance, recurrent resources and an established annual planning cycle from September 2029.
